name: Lithium level increase risk with interacting drugs
actions: Avoid combination when possible or consider alternatives (educational).
If used together, check lithium level after starting or changing the interacting drug and adjust lithium dose as needed (educational).
Monitor for lithium toxicity (tremor, GI upset, ataxia, confusion) and monitor renal function (educational).

rationale: Lithium has a narrow therapeutic index.
The interacting drug can increase lithium levels, increasing toxicity risk.

explanation_template: {A_name} and {B_name} can interact in a way that increases lithium concentration and toxicity risk. Monitor lithium levels and renal function and watch for neurotoxicity symptoms, especially after medication changes (educational).

references.citation: Lithium interactions: NSAIDs, ACE inhibitors, and thiazide diuretics can increase lithium levels.
